Get both convenient and quality care, wherever and whenever you need it, with accessible video visits and direct messaging with your providers.
Care on your terms

No commute, no membership fees, no endless wait times. Get expert care when you need it. Tia's providers deeply understand women's health at every stage, with the experience and credentials to handle both everyday concerns and complex issues, virtually or in-person.
A team of trusted women's health specialists who actually get what you're dealing with

children.text: Primary Care at Tia is women-focused, expanding beyond gynecological practices. We require a
whole-health practice

to broaden the collective definition of women’s health and well-being. Women’s health is a whole lot more than reproductive wellness or gynecology and it necessitates a whole lot more than an annual Pap test.
Whole Women's Health
Women’s health is more than gynecology.

At present, our healthcare system is organized by specialists whose expertise lie not in comprehensive or preventative healthcare, but in the singular management or intervention of disease. This creates a fragmented health system for patients, and particular for women.
In an effort to address this schism Tia is organized to address all of your health needs
under one roof

with your primary care and gynecology fully integrated. With this system we are able to diagnose, treat and prevent disease in women’s health. We do this through cultivating comprehensive women’s health providers who are trained to connect all of the dots that comprise female health and its nuances, complexity and interconnectedness.
Did you know?
Your Primary Care at Tia looks at the whole you, considering your unique milleui of female hormones.
Health as a whole should be the driving force behind women’s healthcare at large. Women’s healthcare cannot operate as a one-size-fits-all, it must be personalized.
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.
Our healthcare system has excellent programs for disease-specific interventions and population-specific medical residencies, like geriatrics, but none focused on women’s health end-to-end.

children.text: What is a vaginal infection appointment? What is it for?

A vaginal infection appointment is usually a consultation visit and sometimes a physical exam to discuss vaginal symptoms, recommend prevention and offer treatment if indicated. This visit may also include a recommendation for STI testing depending on your symptoms.
Symptoms associated with a vaginal infection
Vaginal infection symptoms can vary but some common indications that an infection might be present include:
The color, amount, odor, or texture of your discharge has changed.
You are experiencing itching or irritation in your vagina or vulva.
There is a burning sensation, especially during sex or when urinating.
Your vulva is swelling or has increased redness.
You are experiencing vaginal pain and soreness.
When should you schedule a vaginal infection appointment?
Many women might have been
previously diagnosed

with a vaginal infection at least once, meaning you probably know the signs and symptoms to look out for and often can self diagnose your infection. Sometimes, a over the counter medication can ease the symptoms, but will not always cure the infection. You should schedule a vaginal infection visit if:
Did you know?
Bacterial Vaginitis (BV) is the most common vaginal condition in women 15-44, affects 29% of women in this age group.
You have taken a full course of over the counter medication and your symptoms are still present.
You have a history of chronic vaginal infections.
This is the first time you are experiencing any of the symptoms listed above -- Or if a new symptom has appeared.
You've had multiple sexual partners or you recently introduced a new partner.
You have fever, chills, or pelvic pain.
Even if you have been
diagnosed with a vaginal infection before
and it might feel pretty routine to cure it yourself, it is always advised to see medical attention. Your Tia provider will help you make an informed decision with your treatment plan.
Did you know?

Vaginal candidiasis (yeast infection) is the second most common type of vaginal infection after bacterial vaginal infections. An estimated 1.4 million outpatient visits for vaginal candidiasis occur annually.
What to expect during a vaginal infection appointment

If your symptoms are typical to what you've experienced in the past, a virtual visit might suffice to accurately asses the situation and perscribe any medications needed. During a virtual visit, your provider will ask about your symptoms, medical history, and any current medications you are taking. Your provider might recommend over-the-counter or self-care techniques to help manage your symptoms. Additionally, antifungal or antibiotic medications might be perscribed to treat the infection. In some cases, your provider will recommend additional testing to confirm your symptoms. But this is not always necessary.

Tia members can also schedule an in-person vaginal infection appointment. Your provider will review your symptoms prior to performing a pelvic exam with a swab collection. This sample will then be sent to the lab for confirmation of diagnosis, which can take up to 24 days. In some cases, your provider can treat you simply based on a visual examination -- in efforts to relieve your symptoms -- while awaiting lab confirmation.
Potential treatment plans for vaginal infections
There are a variety of ways to treat a vaginal infection depending on the diagnosis and severity. Your provider might recommend/perscribe:
Lifestyle/Diet Changes
Lowering saturated fat intake:
Consuming lots of saturated fat can increase the pH (aka acidity) of your vagina which increases the risk of Bacterial vaginosis. Try adding more olive oil, nuts, and avocados into your diet.
Increaseing fiber intake:
Fiber is an amazing prebiotic. This means it stimulates the growth of and maintenance of desired gut bacteria. Incorporate more onions, bananas, garlic, apples and oats into your diet.
Vitamin D:
Low levels of Vitamin D have been associated with a higher risk of Bacterial vaginosis. We recommend starting with 2000 IU daily.
Probiotics:
Probiotics, either supplements or whole foods, may improve your symptoms and prevent a potential infection. We like adding more yogurt, sauerkraut, miso, pickles and kimchi into your diet.
Medications
Monistat:
This is an over-the-counter antifungal ointment that can treat yeast infections.
Boric acid:
This weak acid

may stop bacteria or yeast from continuing to grow. It may also help in restoring your vaginal acidity and re-establish the ideal environment for beneficial vaginal flora to return. It is important to only use it as a vaginal suppository as it can be fatal if taken orally.
Topical or oral perscription medications:
There are a variety of different medications that might be perscribed depending on the diagnosis.
Vaginal Health Recommendations
Considering a menstrual cup:
There may be a reduced association of using a menstrual cup and Bacterial vaginosis and Candidiasis infections.
Yin care:

Yin Care is a combination of anti-pathogenic Chinese herbs that can be used to help deal with signs and symptoms of vaginal “yeast” (bacterial or fungal infections). You can use the solution (diluted) as a sitz bath, herbal wash, or soak.
In some cases, your provider might recommend a follow up visit for additional screening.
How can you prevent future vaginal infections?

Normal and abnormal variations in vaginal discharge should be routinely discussed with your provider. Additionally, ask your provider to help educate you on common vaginal infections. These infections are not sexually transmitted but rather an imbalance of the vaginal pH.

Common causes of vaginal infections can include prolonged time in sweaty or wet clothing, non-cotton underwear, changes in mental or physical health, scented hygiene products or detergents. Although these infections are not STIs, some patients notice symptoms following sexual activity.
You can help keep your vagina healthy by:
Avoiding douching
Washing the area at least once daily with unscented, plain soap or even just water
Avoiding scented personal care products in your vaginal area
Avoiding perfumed feminine hygiene sprays and deodorants
Practicing safe sex by using a condom every time you have intercourse
Wiping from front to back after using the bathroom
Getting regular gynecological checkups
Taking a daily probiotic
Following treatment, your symptoms should clear completly. Follow up testing and treatment is not generally needed.
Experiencing symptoms? Sign up for Tia's membership and schedule your vaginal infection appointment.

children.text: Birth Control - Contraception Counseling
Your First Step to Finding the Right Birth Control

items: Share your goals, preferences, and history
Talk through all your options, including the pill, IUD, patch, ring, implant, and more.
Get a prescription or plan for your next steps

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is this appointment?
We listen to your needs
Your provider will listen to your current symptoms, concerns, and goals to understand the full picture of your health.
We review your health history
We'll go over your medical, birth control and period history to help inform your options.
We build a personalized contraception plan

We build a personalized contraception plan. Your provider will partner with you to explore a full range of options, including cycle tracking, the pill, IUD, patch, ring, or implant, to find the best fit for your body, lifestyle, and goals.
Who should have a Birth Control Consult?
You should book this appointment if you're looking to start a new birth control method, need to renew a current prescription, or want to switch to a different type of contraception.
Symptoms we care for
If you have never taken birth control before (or simply took a break from taking it), there are several reasons and symptoms that might make you a good candidate for contraception:
Pregnancy prevention
Irregular periods
Heavy or painful periods
Missed or skipped periods
Managing the symptoms of PCOS, like acne or hair growth
How the Birth Control appointment can help
This appointment can help you:
Make an informed decision:
Get clarity on the pros, cons, and effectiveness of different birth control methods, so you can choose what feels right for you.
Get a clear roadmap:
Create a practical plan for your next steps, whether that's a prescription for the pill, a separate appointment for an IUD insertion, or guidance on non-hormonal options.
Explore a wide range of options:
Learn about hormonal and non-hormonal contraception, as well as the latest medical advancements in the field

cells:
Your provider places it under the skin of your upper arm. It has to be removed by your provider.

Effectiveness: >99%

Pros: Good for five years. You may not have a period at all.

Cons:It is possible to bleed for longer than desired amounts of time and the bleeding can be unpredictable.

Must be placed in the uterus by your provider. Your provider also needs to remove it.

Effectiveness: >99%

Pros: Effective for up to 12 years depending on which IUD you choose. Hormonal IUDs can decrease or stop bleeding. The copper IUD does not have any hormones.

Cons: You need an appt to get the IUD inserted and removed. Paragard, the 12-year IUD, can increase bleeding.

Apply a new patch once a week for three weeks. No patch needed for week four.

Effectiveness: >93%

Pros: No need to take a pill every single day.

Cons: Depending where it’s placed on the body, it can be visible. Not ideal for swimmers or those who have increased sweating.

Insert a small ring into the vagina each month or annually depending on the ring of choice.

Effectiveness: >93%

Pros: Easy to start and stop when desired.

Cons: You need to remove and replace the ring. Some might want to remove it for sex, though it can stay in the vagina during sex.

Your provider will give you a shot every three months (13 weeks).

Effectiveness: >96%

Pros: You only need to think about it every three months.

Cons: It’s a shot! You need to come to the clinic every three months for it.

Pros: You can control your bleeding by continuously cycling. You can start and stop the pill easily.

Cons: You need to remember to take it every day.

children.text: Your Birth Control Appointment: What to Expect
Preparing for Your Appointment
Before your appointment, we recommend updating your medical history, including any past or current medications. Think about your goals for contraception and any questions you have.
During Your Appointment

You’ll meet with one of Tia’s providers who is well versed in contraception options. Your provider will review your medical history, period history, medication history, and contraception goals. You'll also have a discussion about the pros, cons, and effectiveness of each birth control option, as well as potential risks or side effects.
After Your Appointment
You’ll leave your appointment with a clear plan for next steps. These may include:
Prescription:
If you choose a method like the pill, patch, ring, or shot, your provider can send a prescription to your pharmacy.
Procedure:
If you and your provider decide on an IUD or implant, your Care Coordinator will help you schedule a separate in-person appointment for the placement.
Continued Support:
If you have any questions, your care team will be available to support.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! We will bill your insurance for this visit.
Our experts in Birth Control
Where to get an appointment
Book an appointment

Choosing the right birth control is an important part of caring for your health and planning your future. A birth control counseling appointment helps you explore your options, from pills and patches to long-acting methods like IUDs and implants, so you can find what fits your body, your lifestyle, and your goals with personalized guidance from your care team.

children.text: Perimenopause and Menopause Assessment

items: A holistic approach to a whole-body transition
Support for symptoms like hot flashes & brain fog
Expert care, from HRT to acupuncture
Your choices, supported by experts

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
How do I know if a Perimenopause and Menopause Assessment is right for me?
What is a Perimenopause & Menopause Assessment visit?

The Perimenopause & Menopause Assessment is a comprehensive, whole-body visit with one of Tia’s menopause care specialists. We'll listen to your concerns, review your health history, and work with you to create a personalized care plan for your unique journey through perimenopause or menopause, all with your preferences at the center.
Who should get a  Perimenopause & Menopause Assessment?

Whether you’re curious if you’re in perimenopause or certain you’re in menopause, we are here to care for you. Our holistic approach means that you're always in the driver's seat of your healthcare decisions, no matter where you may be:
Early Perimenopause: When symptoms first begin and may be subtle.
Late Perimenopause: When symptoms become more frequent or severe.
Menopause: When you have not had a period for 12 consecutive months.
Post-Menopause: The years following menopause, where new health concerns can arise.
Symptoms we can support
We provide expert, whole-body care for a wide range of perimenopause and menopause symptoms, including:
Hot flashes
Sleep disturbances
Brain fog and memory issues
Mood changes, anxiety, or irritability
Low libido
Weight changes
Vaginal dryness
Bone health concerns
Cardiometabolic concerns
How this appointment can help:
Understand what’s happening —
connect the dots between your symptoms and this life stage, exploring root causes
Build a personalized plan —
from hormone therapy to acupuncture, with your preferences guiding every decision
Get clear, expert answers —
evidence-based guidance and myth-busting from women’s health specialists
Feel heard and supported —
a care team that listens, validates, and partners with you on next steps
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! Tia will bill your insurance. Just like at any doctor’s office, you may be responsible for a co-pay or coinsurance, depending on your health plan.
Our experts in perimenopause and menopause care

children.text: Book same-week women’s perimenopause & menopause care
Hormonal shifts during perimenopause and menopause can bring changes in sleep, mood, and body. In this visit, we’ll review your symptoms and create a plan to help you feel balanced and supported.

children.text: What is a skin care appointment?
An acne, rash, or skin change visit at Tia can be facilitated in clinic or virtually and is conducted by an MD or nurse pracitioner.
When should you schedule a skin care appointment?
It is recommended to schedule an appointment to discuss skin changes if:
Your symptoms are not responding to over the counter treatment.
Your skin changes are associated with the menstrual cycle.
Your symptoms are becoming bothersome or are worsening over time.
What to expect during a skin care appointment
Here’s what you can expect during a Tia dermatology appointment:
A comprehensive assessment:
Your provider will factor in your skin concerns, overall health, and well-being to create your personalized treatment plan.
A customized Care Plan:

Based on your assessment, your provider will work with you to develop a personalized treatment plan. This may include a combination of medications, lifestyle recommendations, holistic therapies like acupuncture, or other interventions aimed at optimizing your skin health.
Follow-up care:
Seeing changes in your skin can take time, and our team will be there every step of the way to make sure your Care Plan is working.

After your appointment, we'll provide clear guidance on next steps. Whether it's booking a follow-up, adjusting your treatment plan, or providing ongoing support, we'll ensure you get the comprehensive care you deserve.
Emotional support:

Skin concerns can bring up a lot of feelings, and we're here to listen, provide support, and put you at ease. At Tia, care is collaborative. You’re always welcome to ask questions, express concerns, or discuss any care preferences with your provider.
Potential treatment plans for skin issues
Once your provider has conducted a thorough medical exam and reviewed your medical history associated with your skin issues, there are several treatment options they might recommend:
Depending on your symptoms, your provider might recommend additional lab testing.
Your provider might recommened keeping a symptom journal to track when, how often, and for how long your symptoms appear.
Depending on symptoms and severity, ertain over the counter medication can be recommended or your provider will perscribe medication.
If your provider suspects that your symptoms are more severe, you might recieve a referral to a dermatology specialist.
Got skin issues? Join Tia and schedule an appointment virtualy or at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

modules.title: Get to know dermatology at Tia

main.shortDescription: Struggling with breakouts? We connect the dots between your hormones, stress, diet, and more to help you manage your skin in connection to your whole health. Dealing with redness, itchiness, swelling, or change of color/texture of your skin? Book a consultation to uncover what's going on. If you are experiencing active rash or skin changes, please book an in clinic appointment so we can take a look.

children.text: Let's refresh - What is a pap smear?
During a Pap smear, a sample of cervical cells is collected and examined under a microscope.
An abnormal result

indicates that there are changes in the size, shape, or appearance of the cervical cells, which could be indicative of infections, inflammation, precancerous, or cancerous conditions. Further diagnostic tests or procedures may be recommended based on the degree and nature of the abnormalities to determine the appropriate course of action for the individual's health. Regular Pap smears are essential for early detection and prevention of cervical cancer.
Did you know?

About 5% of all Pap tests will be abnormal, meaning that the sample contains atypical cervical cells. However, the majority of these cells are not cancerous or even precancerous. An abnormal Pap test result does not mean cancer, but it does require follow-up to rule out the possibility of cancer.
When to schedule an appointment regarding an abnormal pap smear result
This visit type might be recommended if:
You have a history of abnormal pap smears and want to talk about screening recommendations.
Your
pap results
at Tia are abnormal and you want to discuss further.
You have completed abnormal pap follow-up (colposcopy, LEEP) and want to discuss next steps and screening recommendations.
What to expect during your appointment

This is a consultation based visit to review previous pap smear results and discuss recommendations. It is very helpful if previous results are available and that they be uploaded to your Tia provider ahead of this visit.
Potential treatment plans and next steps
Once your provider has reviewed your
previous pap smear results

and discussed your concerns and questions, your provider will provide potential updated cervical cancer screening recommendations which may include future pap recommendations, an appointment for a pap smear or a colposcopy. In some cases, this may require referral to a gynecological surgical specialist.
Did you know?
Abnormal Pap tests are very common. In fact, of the 3 million women with abnormal Pap tests each year, less than 1% (13,240 cases) will be diagnosed with cervical cancer.
Questions about your pap test? Join Tia's membership and schedule an appointment to discuss your options at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

main.shortDescription: Unsure what your abnormal Pap means? We’ll explain everything and work with you in designing appropriate treatment and follow-ups.
main.title: Abnormal Pap

children.text: PCOS Treatment

items: Whole-person care for managing symptoms of Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome (PCOS)
Care that looks at the root cause, from hormonal imbalances to insulin resistance
Personalized plans based on your unique goals, including if and when you want to conceive
Ongoing, integrated support for a chronic condition, with care that sees you as a whole person—not just a diagnosis

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
PCOS Consultation: What to Expect?
Comprehensive PCOS diagnosis and assessment

Your journey begins with a comprehensive assessment to discuss your current symptoms, medical history, and family risk factors. We take a deep dive into your period history and pregnancy goals to build a complete picture of your health. This visit may also result in orders for bloodwork or an ultrasound to confirm a diagnosis.
Building your personalized PCOS treatment plan

Together, we'll create a personalized care plan to manage your specific symptoms. You and your provider will determine the best path for you based on your symptoms, overall health, and personal goals. We’ll explore all of your options, including lifestyle changes, supplements, and medication.
Continuing support for long-term PCOS management

PCOS is a chronic condition, and we’re here for the long term. Your Care Team provides continuous, relationship-based support to track your progress, adjust your plan as needed, and ensure your care is comprehensive, not piecemeal.
Who should have a PCOS appointment?
This visit is for anyone with a PCOS diagnosis or symptoms that may point to PCOS.
Symptoms we care for:
Irregular, long, or absent periods
Excess facial or body hair growth
Acne
Weight gain or difficulty losing weight
Difficulty getting pregnant
How the PCOS appointment can help
This care is designed to empower you with a compassionate and comprehensive plan to manage your symptoms and improve your quality of life.
This assessment helps you:
Understand your body better
through a comprehensive evaluation of your hormonal and metabolic health.
Explore all your options
in a judgment-free space, from lifestyle changes to medication.
Create a personalized care plan
tailored to your unique symptoms and goals.
Manage your PCOS symptoms effectively
with a team that supports you without shame or blame.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! Tia will bill your insurance. Just like at any doctor’s office, you may be responsible for a co-pay or coinsurance, depending on your health plan.
Potential treatment plans for PCOS

There is no cure for PCOS but there are many options to manage your symptoms. Your provider will determine the best treatment for you based on your symptoms, medical history, and overall health, as well as your desire to conceive. Treatment options may include medication, lifestyle changes, or a combination of both.

cells: This medication suppresses androgens which can improve the symptoms of unwanted hair growth and acne.
This diabetes drug is believed to work by improving insulin sensitivity, which is commonly associated with PCOS. By lowering your insulin level, metformin allows many women to cycle more normally.

For those who do not want to become pregnant, treating PCOS often involves taking hormonal birth control to manage the symptoms of PCOS, like painful periods, acne, and excess body hair. The pill, the patch, and the ring work by preventing ovulation which reduces the number of cysts on the ovary.
Is thought to improve insulin sensitivity, lower blood pressure and decrease inflammation.
Inositol may help reduce blood triglyceride levels, improve insulin function, lower blood pressure and promote ovulation in women with PCOS.
Vitamin D promotes insulin sensitivity in addition to helping with calcium absorption.
Zinc may improve insulin resistance, decrease cramping with periods, unwanted hair growth and acne.
This is a phytonutrient derived from vegetables that blocks androgen receptors which can help with acne and unwanted hair growth.
This has significant anti-androgen effects and contains compounds called polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties.

The peony and licorice (Shao Yao Gan Cao Tang) combination has undergone clinical trials in which it was found to reduce testosterone and improve cycle regularity. Caution with high blood pressure as licorice may raise blood pressure.
Aim to get at least 25 grams of fiber per day.
Healthy fats can help sustain your energy and support healthy cholesterol levels.
Try more restorative forms of exercise rather than high intensity.
Acupuncture can help to regulate the cycle, encourage healthy ovulation, reduce signs of androgen excess, and support metabolism.

Passionate about reproductive justice and cultivating nonjudgemental medical spaces for all bodies, Allison uses active listening, empathy, sex-positivity, and humor to establish and maintain relationships with Tia members. They have extensive experience working in marginalized communities (e.g., those living with mental illness/ substance use disorders, abuse survivors, LGBTQ+ individuals, immigrants and their first generation family members) and are particularly excited to use Tia's innovative virtual platform to increase access in these spaces.

modules.title: How virtual care at Tia is different

children.text: Nope! We have not figured out how to do a Pap on zoom (and we don't foresee this happening anytime soon either), but we can treat your UTI, yeast infection, review your health history, track the source of your pelvic pain, and so much more virtually — and, as needed, you can visit our in-person clinic for the physical exams.

faqs.question: Are you doing gynecology exams over the internet?

children.text: They are very similar. Telehealth typically means a one-time video or phone visit.
Virtual care

at Tia is more than that — it’s a connected care experience. From messaging with your provider to managing prescriptions and follow-ups, we make it easy to get the care you need and want. Think of it as your care team, just a tap away.

faqs.question: Is virtual care the same as telehealth?

children.text: No, the quality of care doesn’t change. Every virtual service we offer has been intentionally selected because we know we can deliver it with the same level of clinical quality, personalization, and compassion as in person. You’re in good hands, wherever you are.

faqs.question: Is the quality of care different between virtual or in person services?

children.text: Tia will make thoughtful recommendations when in-person care is needed.

faqs.question: What if I need an in-person appointment?

to help navigate specialists, prescriptions & lab work
Soon

have the added layer of care team curation, a personal health navigator, and more robust between visit support, which may be especially helpful for those navigating chronic conditions, in particular life stages, or who simply want someone else to help guide their health.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s primary care services may be right for you if you:
Need a primary care provider or are overdue for an annual exam
Have a common illness like a cough, cold, flu, or a UTI.
Experiencing headaches, body aches, or unexplained pain.
Need support managing a chronic condition like high blood pressure or diabetes.
Feel anxious, overwhelmed, or "off," and you're not sure why.
Need a prescription refilled or want to discuss medication management.
Have ongoing issues like gut problems or trouble sleeping.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

testimonials.Content: I cannot tell you how much I truly valued my experience here at this clinic. From the minute I walked in, I felt safe, comfortable and welcomed. The exam room had so many amenities to make you feel at home, I have a hard time calling it a "exam room" - they had amazing robes to wear, drinks/snacks just in case, and even a little sitting area in the room. My nurse was the most incredible woman and held my hand throughout the exam...everything was just perfect. I highly recommend Tia to every woman who wants to have a better doctor experience. This is unlike anything I have experienced and will be recommending every woman I know to Tia!
testimonials.Title: Jessica O., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: It's always easy and I've never felt so taken care of in my life! I actually don't dread making appointments and I don't have to put on armor when I go see my doctor. It's a game changer!
testimonials.Title: Stephanie S., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I can’t recommend Tia enough. As a woman who doesn’t like going to the doctor (white coat syndrome) and has been discounted and not listened to by doctors in the past, this experience is everything a doctors appt should be. I am so happy I am now a Tia patient and would highly recommend Tia to any female friend or family member that could benefit from the amazing staff and care here 💕
testimonials.Title: Katherine E. Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I’ve never experienced such personalized, sensitive, thorough, and holistic women’s healthcare in my life. So grateful for this clinic!

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for Sexual and Gynecological health
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Your primary care provider is always in sync with your GYN care, so you never have to repeat your history to get a Pap test, STI screen, or tackle a more complex condition.
✓ Seamless Specialty Access
Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care
We use both medication and integrative options (like acupuncture or pelvic floor therapy referrals) for chronic conditions like PCOS or painful sex, aiming to get to the root and give you options.
✓ Care that grows with you
We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted

testimonials.Content: My experience at Tia was exceptional from start to finish. As someone with a 9–5 job, being able to book a 6 PM appointment for an OBGYN concern was a huge relief. From check-in, Angie made me feel welcomed and cared for, and Stephanie was professional, gentle with the blood draw, and made sure I felt comfortable the whole time. I saw Jamee, the nurse practitioner, who was incredibly supportive and knowledgeable. She took her time answering all my questions and helped me feel at ease with clear explanations and genuine care. Tia delivers warm, respectful, patient-focused care, and I’ve already started recommending it to friends and colleagues. Here, you truly feel heard and supported.
testimonials.Title: Clarissa B., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I had my IUD removed and replaced today which is probably one of the most uncomfortable procedures EVER but the care team made sure that my comfort was prioritized. In addition to the luxurious robe and beautiful interiors I've come to love from Tia, I was provided with a heating pad, white noise machine, and lavender essential oil to smell during my procedure. My NP was extremely thorough and asked if I had any questions and walked me through every step as it was happening. I felt very taken care of! As soon as I got home I was messaged aftercare information! It’s clear that Tia thinks through every step of their care and prioritizes comfort along with exceptional healthcare.
testimonials.Title: Chloe M., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: Amazing experience with Tia! The providers are compassionate and the office beautiful and efficient. The care is excellent with a focus on the whole person, offering mental health and hormone treatments as well as primary women's healthcare. Used acupuncture with my procedure and couldn't believe the difference it made. 10/10 recommend Tia to anyone
testimonials.Title: Alexis R.,Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I’ve read many reviews about this facility, but my experience was truly exceptional. I’ve had a lot of trauma around women’s health and avoided gynecologic care for years, but Tia completely changed that. Dr. Ryan Darvish is the first provider who made me feel genuinely safe and cared for. After an abnormal pap, I went through several difficult procedures, and he guided me through each one with compassion, clear communication, and a focus on keeping me comfortable. The same care and support were present during my IUD removal and replacement, which he made nearly painless. I’ve never felt so at ease during appointments that once felt overwhelming. Dr. Darvish is truly a gift, and I recommend him to anyone with fear or trauma around gynecologic care. Tia is now the only place I’ll go for my health needs.
testimonials.Title: Emily F., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I cannot say enough good things about my experience at Tia Clinic in Studio City, thanks to Jamee and Alauna. Their customer service was truly unmatched—every interaction was thoughtful, kind, and professional. Jamee was extremely patient with me, taking the time to answer every single one of my many questions without hesitation. She made me feel heard and valued, which is so rare these days. Both Jamee and Alauna went above and beyond to make me feel comfortable throughout my visit.  I’ve officially found my gyno for life with Jamee, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the care and support I received. If you’re looking for a team that genuinely cares about their patients, look no further than Tia Clinic.
testimonials.Title: Tia H., Tia Patient

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Mental healthcare can feel fragmented, separating your mind from your body. When you feel overwhelmed, anxious, or like "something is off," you may be told it's "just stress" without a look at your hormones, sleep, digestion, or other causes. This leaves you managing symptoms without understanding
why
you feel this way, especially problematic for women who face disproportionately high rates of anxiety and depression compared to men.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right weight & metabolic health doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution for Women

Our mental health care starts with treating your body, not just your brain. Our expert-led whole-person approach looks at the full picture of your health—including the physiological effects of stress, hormones, and sleep—to find the root cause. You get a connected, relationship-based approach with an expanded toolkit, including care from specialty trained primary care providers and psychiatric specialists if needed. We consider mental health a core part of your physical health, ensuring consistent, holistic, and long-term support.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right weight & metabolic health doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Mental Health support may be right for you if you’re:
Feeling anxious or overwhelmed all the time
Noticing symptoms of depression or sadness that won't lift
Feeling chronic stress, burnout, or irritability
Struggling with sleep issues or brain fog that are affecting your daily life
Experiencing hormone-related mood changes or feeling "off" after a life stage like postpartum
Navigating a challenging life stage
Curious about exploring whole-body treatment options, including labs, medication, or acupuncture

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right weight & metabolic health doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Mental Health matters for women
The Connection is Read: Your Mind
is
Your Body

You deserve to feel like yourself again. The belief that mental health and physical health are separate has led to a fragmented system where patients are often left to piece together care on their own. This is frustrating, especially since the impact of mental health on chronic conditions like heart disease, diabetes, and stroke is significant and preventable. Our model is designed to support you by acknowledging that your mental, physical, and reproductive health are inextricably linked.
When “Just Stressed” Gets Overlooked
Women experience anxiety and depression at nearly
twice

the rate of men, yet stress and mood changes are often hard to fully assess in today’s fragmented healthcare system. Key contributors, like hormones, sleep, pain, and life context, can go unrecognized, especially when national depression screening rates in primary care hover around 39%.
Our integrated approach is designed to make it easier to catch what’s really going on. That’s why over
of our patients receive evidence-based depression screening.
A Model Build for the Long Term

Mental health care shouldn't be limited to just one solution. Tia's integrated model is designed to empower you with personalized care, offering a full suite of options from clinical to subclinical modalities—like medication, acupuncture, or supplements. By connecting your primary care with specialized psychiatric support when needed, we ensure you have a collaborative Care Team providing a personalized path toward feeling like your whole self again.e.

“We're here to support our patients in making the decisions that are right for them. Whatever you’re going through, I want you to know: Tia is here to support you feeling better—physically and emotionally. Because improved mental health is improved health.”
Jessica Horwitz, Chief Clinical Officer
How Tia is different for Mental Health
Tia's approach treats your mental well-being as a core part of your physical health, not something separate.
✓ We Connect Mind, Body, and Hormones

Our providers are specialty trained to look at the whole-system effects of stress across your sleep, digestion, hormones, and more. To ensure we get to the root of your experience, together we may consider additional labs and testing for your unique situation.
✓ An Expanded, Expert-Led Toolkit

You get comprehensive care from specialty trained primary care providers, backed by psychiatric mental health nurse practitioners as needed. Your plan offers a wide range of solutions beyond medication—from acupuncture and supplements to holistic planning and nutrition counseling.
✓ Support Designed for Women's Life Stages

We understand that anxiety and depression rates are disproportionately high for women and often surface during major life transitions. We offer care for women navigating hormonal shifts, postpartum recovery, and perimenopause, providing consistent follow-up and long-term support.

testimonials.Content: It’s a relief to be genuinely cared for as a whole human—physically, mentally, emotionally. I feel like more than the sum of my worst symptoms, and my smallest issues are not only heard, but tracked and revisited.
testimonials.Title: Katheryn S., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: My stress was out of this world and significantly impacting my sleep. The care, thoughtfulness, and deep listening my provider offered was immediately reassuring. I left with a comprehensive plan that I could start that day. She demonstrated a complete understanding of what I was and was not open to trying, and never pressured me into a direction that wasn't right for me.
testimonials.Title: Tia Mental Health Patient

children.text: Book a Mental Health Assessment

Mental health affects how you think, feel, and show up in every part of your life, from relationships and work to sleep and physical wellbeing. Whether you’re dealing with anxiety, low mood, stress that feels unmanageable, or just want support understanding your emotional patterns, Tia’s care team offers thoughtful, evidence-based guidance to help you make sense of what you’re experiencing. We’ll look at your history, habits, and triggers to understand the root cause, then co-create a plan that supports your mind, builds resilience, and helps you feel grounded, capable, and more like yourself again.

children.text: Alena is a Nurse Practitioner with over 13 years of experience working with LGBTQ+ and BIPOC patients of all backgrounds. Her goal is to inspire confidence in patients so they can take charge of their health and ultimately achieve realistic health goals to have the highest quality of life available to them. She leverages a holistic approach that marries traditional and alternative medicine to optimize patient health, striving to give all patients the tools to improve emotional as well as physical health.
